articles.title: At CPAC, the ‘America First’ Crowd Ponders Giving War a Chance
articles.summary: The Conservative Political Action Conference has been an America First gathering for years, but with President Trump pursuing war in Iran, organizers have tried to make a shift, with mixed results.

articles.title: House Vote Sets Up Clash With the Senate on D.H.S. Funding, Prolonging Shutdown
articles.summary: Republicans revolted over a Senate measure to fund the Department of Homeland Security and passed a rival bill, dimming the chances of a quick end to the crisis crippling airports.

articles.title: Florida’s Immigration Crackdown, Led by DeSantis, Is Showing Cracks
articles.summary: Some conservative sheriffs have raised concerns about the aggressive enforcement tactics that Gov. Ron DeSantis, a Republican, has embraced.

articles.title: U.S. Antitank Mines Discovered in Neighborhood in Iran
articles.summary: Photos and video verified by The New York Times show mines dispensed by cluster bombs in a village a few miles from a missile site.

articles.title: How Long of a Wait at Security? For Many Passengers, It Was Anyone’s Guess.
articles.summary: As T.S.A. staff shortages continued on Friday, some airports saw interminably long wait times, while others barely had a wait at all, adding to travelers’ confusion.

articles.title: Man arrested in plot to firebomb Palestinian activist's home after undercover op
articles.summary: An official who was briefed on the investigation said Alexander Heifler, 26, identified as a member of the JDL 613 Brotherhood, which describes its membership as "Jewish warriors" fighting back agains

articles.title: The Justice Department plans to share sensitive voter data with Homeland Security
articles.summary: The Justice Department has sought voter data from states. It now says it plans to share that data with the Department of Homeland Security, to run it through a controversial citizenship check tool.

articles.title: Tiger Woods arrested on suspicion of DUI, authorities say
articles.summary: Woods was arrested on suspicion of DUI after he struck another vehicle and rolled his Land Rover, not far from where he lives on Jupiter Island, Florida. The sheriff's office said he was not injured.

articles.title: Cannonball with Wesley Morris: ‘Love Story’ Is Actually a Horror Story
articles.summary: The nightmare began when she said, “I do.”

articles.title: Cuban Americans face scrutiny for sending goods back to the island
articles.summary: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.

articles.title: Stocks Keep Falling as Investor Lose Patience With the War in Iran
articles.summary: The S&P 500 is down nearly 9 percent from a January high. The weekly losing streak is its worst in roughly four years.

articles.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth intervened to stop promotions of Black and female officers
articles.summary: Four Army officers were on track to become one-star generals, NPR confirms. Defense secretary Pete Hegseth's involvement in the promotion process is highly unusual.

articles.title: Research points to how companies could make social media less addictive for teens
articles.summary: Juries in two big cases have affirmed what research is finding: The design of social media platforms is particularly compelling and hard to resist for kids. There are growing calls to change it.

articles.title: How long will the war last? No one knows, and it's making oil prices weird
articles.summary: It's like the "Schrödinger's cat" thought experiment. There are two very different potential realities, and traders don't yet know which one is true.

articles.title: House panel finds Florida Democrat guilty of ethics violations
articles.summary: The House Ethics Committee has found evidence that Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick violated House rules. This comes after the panel held a rare public hearing to review investigations into allegations

articles.title: Her dad's dementia inspired her to create a guide for family caregivers
articles.summary: Wambūi Karanja of Kenya is "one to watch," says the Alzheimer's Association. Coping with her dad's condition inspired her to develop a training program for families on the art of caregiving.

articles.title: Americans seek redemption at figure skating worlds, just weeks after the Olympics
articles.summary: Figure Skating World Championships in Prague end on Saturday. Americans Amber Glenn and Ilia Malinin are within medals' reach after disappointing finishes at last month's Olympics.

articles.title: Senate votes to fund most of DHS. And, Trump extends Iran's deadline to reopen strait
articles.summary: The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Iranian missile hits an airbase in Saudi Arabia, injuring U.S. troops
articles.summary: A U.S. official said several U.S. service members were wounded in the strike and some aircraft were apparently damaged as well. Iran has targeted U.S. bases throughout the region since the war began.

articles.title: Want More ‘Love Story’? Read These Books Inspired by the Kennedys and ’90s New York.
articles.summary: If the TV show has you craving 1990s glam, upper-crust romance and doomed dynasties, these books have got you covered.

articles.title: Rubio meets with other G7 nations for first time since Iran war
articles.summary: Secretary of State Rubio meets his G7 colleagues in Paris for the first time since the start of the war on Iran.

articles.title: Southeast Asia is being hit hard by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas
articles.summary: Southeast Asia is among the areas hardest hit by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, with many nations almost entirely dependent on foreign energy — and quickly running out.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
